exploration_log.aha_moment: recovererErrorWriter is the package-level writer that PrintPrettyStack uses. Writing to it just before calling PrintPrettyStack prepends the method/path to the output in the same stream.
exploration_log.hindsight: No dead ends; the change was a single line addition.

alternatives: Modify prettyStack.parse to accept method/path parameters
key_decisions.decision: Write to recovererErrorWriter before calling PrintPrettyStack in Recoverer
key_decisions.reasoning: Doesn't change the public PrintPrettyStack signature or the prettyStack internal logic. The simplest surgical change.

search_sequence.reasoning: Task description pointed to this file.
search_sequence.result: The Recoverer middleware calls PrintPrettyStack without any request context. recovererErrorWriter is the shared writer. Writing to it before PrintPrettyStack achieves the prepend.

minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: edited: added fmt.Fprintf line to prepend method and path before PrintPrettyStack

solve_notes: Added `fmt.Fprintf(recovererErrorWriter, "\n%s %s\n", r.Method, r.URL.Path)` immediately before the `PrintPrettyStack(rvr)` call in the `else` branch of the panic recovery handler. This writes the request method and path to the same writer before the stack trace output, while preserving the existing `PrintPrettyStack` public API.
task_text:
Fix Recoverer middleware not including the request method and path in panic output

When a handler panics, the `Recoverer` middleware in
`middleware/recoverer.go` logs the stack trace but does not include
the HTTP method or request path, making it difficult to identify which
request triggered the panic. Fix the recovery handler to prepend
`r.Method` and `r.URL.Path` to the logged output before the stack
trace.

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: exercises the Recoverer middleware panic recovery path, covering the modified else branch

test_selection.test_query: Find tests that verify the behavior of Recoverer in middleware/recoverer.go

thrash_preventing_defs.name: recovererErrorWriter
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: understanding that this is the shared writer that PrintPrettyStack also uses is critical to knowing that writing to it before the PrintPrettyStack call achieves the desired prepend

thrash_preventing_defs.name: PrintPrettyStack
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms PrintPrettyStack writes to recovererErrorWriter and does not accept method/path parameters, guiding the decision to write separately before calling it

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set contains only the edited function. An AI agent would also read recovererErrorWriter (to confirm it is the shared output writer) and PrintPrettyStack (to see its signature and understand it cannot be passed request context) — without these reads, the agent might try modifying PrintPrettyStack's signature instead and need to backtrack.
